title: Platform Engineer, CloudOps Infrastructure
description: **Job Summary** Iambic is building a secure, cloud\-based platform for running our ML\-driven drug\-discovery workflows. You'll build and own the platform that lets us run our drug\-discovery ML workloads reliably and reproducibly: the deployment templates that make standing up a new environment routine, the orchestration layer that runs model training and inference, and the tooling, CI/CD, and observability that keep everything healthy. It's a mix of building new platform capabilities and keeping scientists' workloads running dependably and on time. This is a hands\-on DevOps/CloudOps role as much as a build role: you'll operate and improve the running system day to day, and adapt the platform as our ML researchers introduce new workflows and models. It suits an engineer who treats infrastructure as a product. This position is based out of our new Ireland office. **Key Responsibilities** * Build and maintain standardized, reproducible, secured deployment templates. * Develop and operate the orchestration layer (orchestration workflows, e.g. Prefect) and the GPU\-backed compute paths that run training and inference, on a schedule and on demand. * Own infrastructure\-as\-code, CI/CD pipelines, and the tooling that makes standing up, updating, and tearing down an environment routine and auditable. * Build observability \- metrics, logging, alerting \- that gives a clear picture of system health across environments. * Run and improve the system day to day (DevOps/CloudOps): drive operational practices that emphasize stability, predictability, and low overhead, and partner with the infosec function on infrastructure security posture. * Adapt and extend the platform as ML researchers introduce new workflows and models \- turning new requirements into supported, repeatable capabilities. * Evaluate and integrate orchestration and deployment technologies; prototype, then harden the patterns that work. * Participate in design discussions, code reviews, and documentation. **Qualifications** * Strong Terraform/OpenTofu engineering skills and hands\-on AWS (or comparable cloud) experience. * Production experience with containerization, container orchestration (e.g. ECS), and CI/CD pipelines. * Infrastructure\-as\-code and reproducible environments. * Solid understanding of distributed systems fundamentals. * Strong operational instincts: observability, debuggability, and maintainability. * Experience operating multi\-tenant or large\-fleet platforms preferred. * Experience with workflow orchestration (Prefect, Airflow, Dagster, or similar) and/or GPU compute platforms preferred. * Familiarity with GPU\-backed environments and ML training/inference pipelines preferred. * Awareness of infrastructure security posture and compliance frameworks (e.g. ISO/IEC 27001 or similar) preferred. * Strong written communication and the ability to work effectively in a distributed team. TECH STACK \& ENVIROMENT * Infrastructure\-as\-code (Terraform/OpenTofu \& Terragrunt) * AWS (ECS and related services) * Automated CI/CD with containerized services * PostgreSQL and object storage (S3\) * Python with modern tooling (e.g. uv, pixi) * Workflow orchestration (e.g. Prefect); GPU\-backed compute for ML workloads * FastAPI with a clean service\-layer architecture (business logic isolated from transport) * Agentic coding tools (e.g. Claude Code) as part of day\-to\-day development **About Iambic Therapeutics** Iambic is a clinical\-stage life\-science and technology company developing novel medicines using its AI\-driven discovery and development platform. Based in San Diego and founded in 2020, Iambic has assembled a world\-class team that unites pioneering AI experts and experienced drug hunters. The Iambic platform has demonstrated delivery of new drug candidates to human clinical trials with unprecedented speed and across multiple target classes and mechanisms of action. Iambic is advancing a pipeline of potential best\-in\-class and first\-in\-class clinical assets, both internally and in partnership, to address urgent unmet patient need. Learn more about the Iambic team, platform, pipeline, and partnerships at iambic.ai. MISSION \& CORE VALUES Our mission is to deliver better medicines through innovations in AI\-based discovery technologies. The culture and work at Iambic Therapeutics are profoundly strengthened by the diversity of our people and our differences in background, culture, national origin, religion, sexual orientation, and life experiences. We are committed to building an inclusive environment where a diverse group of talented humans work together to discover therapeutics and create technologies.

title: Power Platform Software Developer
description: We have an exciting Power Platform role with a top company in Dublin. This company offer a great work/ life balance, hybrid remote work and top packages. Experience * Have a broad knowledge of the Power Platform technology stack. * Design, develop, and implement Power Platform solutions to meet business needs * Develop custom connectors and integrations to connect with external systems * Build, test, and deploy Power Apps model\-driven apps * Create and configure Power Automate workflows * Work collaboratively with stakeholders to gather requirements, provide guidance, and communicate project status * Ensure solutions are scalable, maintainable, and adhere to industry best practices * Troubleshoot and debug issues, and provide technical support when needed * Mentor and train junior team members Requirements: * At least 3 years of experience in developing solutions using Power Platform * Strong knowledge of Power Apps and Power Automate * Experience building custom connectors and integrations * Experiencing utilising Azure Resources (ADF, Logic Apps, Functions) would be a plus * Strong analytical and problem\-solving skills * Excellent communication and interpersonal skills * Ability to work independently and as part of a team * Proven ability to lead and mentor junior developers * Microsoft Power Platform certifications are a plus For more info you can contact me at david@db\-recruitment.ie

title: Private Equity Fund Accounting Supervisor
description: A leading global fund services provider is continuing to expand its Private Equity Fund Accounting team in Dublin on the back of strong new business growth. This is a hands\-on fund accounting role suited to someone with around 4\+ years’ experience who is ready to step into, or continue developing at, Supervisor level. The position would suit someone who is already comfortable preparing and reviewing NAVs, taking ownership of their own work, and beginning to support others through review, training and day\-to\-day guidance. You will work across a portfolio of private equity, fund of funds and related alternative fund structures, supporting the delivery of accurate and timely NAVs while gaining exposure to a growing book of new business. **The role will involve:** * Preparing and reviewing NAVs for private equity and alternative fund structures * Reviewing capital activity, including capital calls, distributions, drawdowns and investor allocations * Supporting management fee, performance fee and waterfall calculations * Completing and reviewing cash, position and portfolio reconciliations * Liaising with clients, auditors, investment managers and internal teams * Taking ownership of allocated deliverables and ensuring deadlines are met * Reviewing work prepared by junior team members where required * Supporting new joiners through training, guidance and day\-to\-day knowledge sharing * Playing a hands\-on role in process improvement and new business onboarding **The ideal candidate will have:** * Around 4\+ years’ experience in fund accounting, ideally with exposure to private equity, alternatives or closed\-ended funds * Strong NAV preparation and review experience * Good technical accounting knowledge and attention to detail * The ability to work independently and take ownership of deadlines * Some exposure to reviewing work, training juniors or supporting team members * Strong communication skills and the confidence to liaise with internal and external stakeholders * A positive, hands\-on approach and an interest in developing further at supervisory level This is a strong opportunity for someone looking to build their career in private equity fund accounting with a growing global platform. The role offers hands\-on technical work, exposure to new business, and the chance to develop supervisory experience. For a confidential discussion, please apply or get in touch to learn more.

title: Product Owner – MDR Platforms
description: **Title:** Product Owner – MDR Plaforms **Location:** Dublin\- Onsite **Job Type:** Permanent Full\-Time **Salary:** Negotiable DOE **Minimum Experience Required:** 5 Years **About Us** Integrity360 is a leading independent cybersecurity and PCI specialist operating across Europe, Africa, the Caribbean, and North America. The company has office locations in Ireland, the UK, Bulgaria, Italy, Sweden, Spain, Lithuania, Ukraine, Africa, the Caribbean, and Canada, supported by six Security Operations Centres (SOCs) located in Dublin, Sofia, Madrid, Stockholm, Rome, and Cape Town. With over 780 employees, including more than 585 dedicated cybersecurity professionals, Integrity360 delivers a full suite of professional, support, and managed security services. These span the complete cyber risk lifecycle, from identification and prevention to detection, response, and recovery. Integrity360 supports over 3000 mid\-market and enterprise organisations across sectors including financial services, insurance, government, healthcare, retail, telecommunications, and utilities. At Integrity360, people come first. We invest heavily in learning, development and progression, fostering a dynamic culture where innovation, collaboration and continuous growth are at the heart of what we do. If you're ready to take your cyber security career to the next level, we’d love to hear from you. **Job Role / Responsibilities** As Product Owner, you will lead the development and continuous improvement of Integrity360’s MDR platforms. These platforms power our MDR services, and incorporates our Online Portal, which serves as the single point of interaction for our customers to access information on all their cybersecurity services, view dashboards and reports, control their services, raise and track tickets, and engage with our expert teams. You will work closely with cross\-functional teams, including the Development team and DevOps, to manage the development pipeline and ensure the delivery of a world\-class customer experience. Your knowledge of cybersecurity technology and services will be instrumental in shaping the platform to meet and exceed customer expectations. **Primary Duties/Responsibilities include:** * **Product Vision \& Strategy:** + Define and communicate the vision and roadmap for the Online Portal, ensuring alignment with customer needs and company objectives. + Working with internal stakeholders define the vision \& roadmap for our MDR platforms to enhance and enable delivery of industry leading services. + Translate strategic goals into actionable features and enhancements. * **Backlog Management:** + Own and maintain the product backlog, prioritising features based on business value, customer impact, and development effort. + Compile specification documents to translate user requirements into clear developer/DevOps guidelines + Collaborate with DevOps teams to refine user stories, acceptance criteria, and technical requirements. * **Stakeholder Engagement:** + Act as the primary point of contact for stakeholders, gathering requirements and feedback to shape the product’s direction and for inclusion in specifications. + Coordination with development/DevOps to ensure releases are scheduled etc. + Regularly communicate progress, priorities, and changes to internal and external stakeholders. + Ensuring new features are tested by the respective departments and sign\-off is achieved before going to production. * **Customer\-Centric Design:** + Drive the creation of intuitive, user\-friendly interfaces and seamless user experiences. + Partner with UX/UI designers and developers to iterate on portal features based on customer feedback and analytics. * **Delivery Oversight:** + Work closely with the development team to ensure timely delivery of high\-quality features. + Monitor development progress and resolve any roadblocks or dependencies. * **Cybersecurity Expertise:** + Leverage your knowledge of cybersecurity products and services to ensure the portal effectively showcases our offerings and supports customer needs. + Stay informed about emerging trends in cybersecurity and incorporate relevant innovations into the portal. * **Performance Monitoring:** + Define key performance indicators (KPIs) to measure the success of the Online Portal. Regularly review analytics and user feedback to identify areas for improvement. + **Competencies** * Strong knowledge of cybersecurity technology, services, and solutions. * Experience working with DevOps teams to manage development pipelines and deliver scalable, reliable solutions. * Excellent understanding of Agile methodologies and backlog management tools (e.g., Jira, Azure DevOps). * Exceptional communication and collaboration skills, with the ability to convey complex ideas to technical and non\-technical audiences. Proven ability to prioritise competing demands and make data\-driven decisions. * **Desired Skills and Experience** * Strong knowledge of cybersecurity technology, services, and solutions. * Experience working with Development and DevOps teams to manage development pipelines and deliver scalable, reliable solutions. * Excellent understanding of Agile methodologies and backlog management tools (e.g., Jira, Azure DevOps). * Knowledge of Git (Gitlab and GitHub) * Exceptional communication and collaboration skills, with the ability to convey complex ideas to technical and non\-technical audiences. * Proven ability to prioritise competing demands and make data\-driven decisions. Familiarity with customer portal design, dashboard creation, and reporting tools is a plus. * \#LI\-JL1

title: Production Chef De Partie Pastry/Kitchen
description: **Production Chef De Partie Pastry/Kitchen** **Location:** Avoca Production Bray Business Park Southern Cross Road, Bray, Co. Wicklow, A98 W8P3 **Hours:** Monday to Friday (Full\-Time) Working hours/times may vary during peak trading periods to meet business requirements **Reports To:** Head Pastry Chef / Production Manager A skilled and versatile Pastry \& Savoury Chef with experience working across both pastry and savoury production within a fast\-paced food manufacturing environment. Responsible for producing a wide range of high\-quality sweet and savoury products while maintaining the highest standards of food safety, quality, and consistency. Works flexibly across all production areas as part of one integrated team, supporting daily production requirements and ensuring products are delivered to specification. **Key Responsibilities** * Preparing, cooking, baking, and finishing a variety of pastry and savoury products. * Following recipes, specifications, and production schedules to ensure consistency and quality. * Working across multiple production areas to support business and operational requirements. * Maintaining high standards of food safety, hygiene, and HACCP compliance. * Ensuring products meet quality, presentation, and customer standards. * Monitoring ingredient usage and minimising waste. * Supporting new product development trials and seasonal product launches. * Maintaining a clean, organised, and safe working environment. * Collaborating with colleagues to achieve production targets and deadlines. **Personal Profile** A flexible, reliable, and quality\-focused chef who thrives in a production environment. Strong team player with the ability to adapt to changing priorities, work efficiently under pressure, and contribute positively to a one\-team culture across both pastry and savoury departments. **What We Offer** * Monday to Friday working schedule outside key trading periods * Training and development opportunities. * Supportive team environment. * Opportunity to grow and develop within the business. * Staff meals onsite **If you have a passion for food and are looking to build your career within a professional production environment, we'd love to hear from you.** ***Avoca is an equal opportunities employer***

title: Professional Support Lawyer (Knowledge) — Projects, Infrastructure & Energy
description: **About Our Client** Our client is a leading, progressive law firm looking to welcome a Professional Support Lawyer (Knowledge) to their multi\-disciplinary Projects, Infrastructure \& Energy group. This is a unique, dynamic role that perfectly blends knowledge leadership with active legal practice. You will enjoy the variety of a strategic knowledge role while staying close to live client matters by carrying a manageable, reduced fee\-earning caseload. **The Role Overview** As a critical support hub for a busy and growing practice, you will provide technical know\-how, training, and support across a broad spectrum of areas, including: * Construction \& engineering * Public procurement * Energy \& natural resources * Planning \& environment * Housing \& ESG (including occasional support for the New Homes/Residential Development team) If you are a qualified solicitor who possesses a passion for technical accuracy, communicates complex legal points with clarity, and thrives on helping colleagues succeed, this role offers an exceptional platform for your career. **Key Responsibilities** * **Knowledge Management:** Develop, curate, and continuously improve the group's knowledge systems, document banks, precedents, and template advice across all core practice areas. * **Legal Analysis:** Monitor and analyze Irish and EU legislative, regulatory, and case\-law developments, translating them into practical guidance for fee earners. * **Training \& Professional Support:** Design and deliver internal training and CPD briefings for legal staff at all levels, while mentoring junior fee earners. * **Fee\-Earning Work:** Maintain a defined, manageable caseload of live matters alongside the wider team, billing in line with a reduced fee target that respects your dual responsibilities. * **Cross\-Departmental Collaboration:** Act as the first point of contact for technical queries, and collaborate with knowledge colleagues across the firm to share resources and best practices. * **Business Development:** Contribute to client\-facing articles, briefings, seminars, and thought\-leadership materials to help elevate the group's profile. **Person Specification** **Essential Criteria:** * Qualified Solicitor (Republic of Ireland) with relevant post\-qualification experience in one or more of the group's practice areas. * Strong technical research and analytical skills, with the ability to distil complex legal material into practical advice. * Exceptional drafting skills and an uncompromising eye for detail. * Excellent communication and presentation skills, with the confidence to deliver training. * Highly organized, self\-directed, and collaborative. **Desirable Criteria:** * Previous experience in a Professional Support Lawyer (PSL) or Knowledge role. * Familiarity with standard\-form construction contracts, public procurement procedures, or residential development. * Experience designing and delivering training or CPD programs. * Comfort utilizing legal technology, knowledge systems, and document automation tools. **What’s on Offer?** * **Broad Exposure:** Work across a multi\-disciplinary practice involved in significant Irish infrastructure, energy, and housing projects. * **The Best of Both Worlds:** A rare role that balances strategic knowledge leadership with continued hands\-on legal work. * **Modern Flexibility:** A supportive hybrid working model. Scope for Part Time. * **Reward:** A highly competitive remuneration and benefits package within a collegiate, growth\-oriented environment. If interested, apply directly or reach out to Caitriona Breaw @ Amicus Recruitment for a more detailed conversation.

title: Project Coordination Specialist
description: **The Role:** BnM is hiring Project Coordination Specialists to provide project management and reporting support to the Project Manager to ensure the execution of major infrastructural projects on time and within budget. Working under the guidance of Project Managers and the PMO, the role ensures project activities progress according to schedule, budget, and governance standards, contributing to the company’s low\-carbon and renewable energy goals. The main duties and responsibilities of the role will include the following: * Work with the PMO project team and support the successful delivery of large\-scale infrastructure projects. Assist in the development of project management plans in the areas of scope, schedule, cost, quality, resource, communications, risk, procurement and stakeholder management. Ensure project governance is adhered to and assist with project reporting to senior management and external stakeholders. * Build good working relationship with the project team to ensure that procedures and processes are understood and followed by all. Assist in knowledge transfer across the PMO team and wider BnM Business. * Coordinate project communications including the preparation of meetings, reports and presentations. Administer the document management system and co\-ordinate the transfer of project information across a diverse and multi\-geographical project team. Utilise the online document control software to distribute and monitor documents for approval and ensure satisfactory revision control is maintained. * Monitor and control project activities, gather and summarise the outputs of project work streams to ensure that deliverables are recorded and acted upon; including attending project meetings to record and follow up on agreed actions. Prepare project specific control documentation using available templates, including project programmes, financial tracking, risk registers, issues log and action log. * Partake in public engagement with the project team, ensuring a proactive approach to community engagement in line with BnM policy is pursued on projects under management. Liaise with management and stakeholders to keep them appraised of project status. * Support in the procurement of all goods and services required by the project in accordance with company policy and legislative requirements. Prepare purchase order requisitions, co\-ordinate the review of service contracts and monitor project spend. Generate monthly financial report including comparison to approved budget estimate and forecast costs to completion. * All employees are expected to co\-operate fully with all provisions taken by the company for ensuring Health and Safety, and Welfare of co\-employees and members of the public using the premises. * Proactively participate in the Performance Management process to ensure delivery of own, and the wider team's objectives. **What we're looking for:** The ideal candidate will possess the following qualification, skills, knowledge, and attributes: * Third\-level degree in Engineering, Business, Project Management or a related discipline (Master’s degree advantageous). Post graduate qualification in project management desirable. Minimum 1 years’ experience in co\-ordination role on large scale energy or infrastructure project delivery. * Competency across a broad range project management skills must be demonstrated. Familiarity with energy infrastructure sector in Ireland desirable. * Excellent technical appraisal, report writing and communications skills essential. Professional project management certification (e.g. PMP or equivalent) is desirable. * Proficient in MS Office and MS Project. Good knowledge and proficiency in online sharing platforms such as SharePoint, Share file, ProjectWise etc. is a distinct advantage. **Apply Now** If you're ready to join a team that’s creating real impact, apply on or before **25 Jun 2026\.** **BnM is an equal opportunities employer.**

title: QC Laboratory Apprentice (Dublin)
description: Location: Dublin, Ireland Job reference: R\-254298 Date posted: 06/12/2026 **Introduction to role:** ------------------------- Are you ready to launch a laboratory career while earning a nationally recognized qualification that directly contributes to delivering life\-changing medicines for people with rare conditions? Join us to learn in a GMP and GLP\-certified biopharmaceutical environment while studying toward a Level 6 Higher Certificate in Science as a Laboratory Technician. This two\-year, blended apprenticeship is offered in partnership with IBEC and Technological University Dublin (Tallaght Campus). You will spend three days each week in our Dublin Centre of Excellence laboratories and two days in academic study, with approval from SOLAS. From day one, you will be embedded in a quality team that supports late\-stage and commercial products, guided by a dedicated QC mentor focused on your growth. Can you picture yourself mastering real laboratory techniques that keep our medicines safe and effective while building a strong foundation for your future? **Accountabilities:** --------------------- * Sample Management: Collect, log, and ship samples to ensure the right materials reach the right tests at the right time, maintaining chain\-of\-custody and traceability. * QC Testing Support: Perform pH checks and physical tests to generate reliable data that informs product release and process decisions. * Equipment Readiness: Calibrate and verify instruments daily to keep the laboratory inspection ready and to ensure data integrity. * Laboratory Operations: Maintain glassware and work areas to high standards, enabling efficient, safe, and compliant testing. * Materials and Logistics: Order and track consumables and chemicals so analysts have what they need to keep testing on schedule. * Raw Material Testing: Support incoming material checks to confirm quality prior to manufacturing use, reducing delays and deviations. * Compliance and Documentation: Follow GMP/GLP practices and complete accurate records to meet regulatory expectations and support audits. * Continuous Learning: Apply classroom theory in the lab, seek feedback from your mentor, and progressively take on more complex tasks that broaden your impact across chemistry, biochemistry, immunology, and microbiology workflows. * Patient and Business Impact: Translate precise laboratory work into dependable quality decisions that safeguard patients and sustain supply for those who need our medicines most. **Essential Skills/Experience:** -------------------------------- * A pass or Grade O6 in five leaving certificate subjects, two of which must be maths and a science related subject. * 250 CAO points, and/or its equivalent (between 18 and under 23 years) * Candidates with a FETAC Level 5 qualification in a related field will be considered, * SOLAS Apprentice approval. **Desirable Skills/Experience:** -------------------------------- * Curiosity about laboratory science and commitment to learning in a regulated environment. * Attention to detail, strong organization, and a methodical approach to following procedures. * Basic familiarity with laboratory safety, good documentation practices, or quality systems. * Comfortable using standard office and data tools; willing to learn laboratory software. * Effective communication and teamwork skills; openness to feedback and coaching. * Any prior hands\-on experience in a school, college, or industrial lab setting. **Why AstraZeneca:** -------------------- Here, your work connects to a clear purpose—advancing medicines for people with rare and devastating diseases. You will learn alongside experienced scientists in state\-of\-the\-art labs, with mentoring that helps you grow from foundational tasks to confident, independent contributions. Expect unexpected teams in the same room unleashing bold thinking—quality specialists, analytical scientists, and manufacturing partners collaborating to solve real problems at speed. We balance high standards with support, valuing kindness alongside ambition, and we invest in your development with a pathway that can progress to a Level 7 degree. Your precision and care will help protect patients and keep vital therapies moving. **Call to Action:** ------------------- Start building a meaningful laboratory career—bring your curiosity and commitment and help us deliver quality medicines that matter. Apply Now! **Date Posted** 12\-Jun\-2026**Closing Date** 25\-Jun\-2026Our mission is to build an inclusive and equitable environment. We want people to feel they belong at AstraZeneca and Alexion, starting with our recruitment process. We welcome and consider applications from all qualified candidates, regardless of characteristics. We offer reasonable adjustments/accommodations to help all candidates to perform at their best. If you have a need for any adjustments/accommodations, please complete the section in the application form.

title: Quality Auditor
description: **Quality Control Auditor \| Dublin 12** **€45,000 \- €55,000 \+ Package** Our client is seeking an experienced Quality Control Auditor / Assessor to support the delivery of high\-quality construction projects across Dublin. The ideal candidate will have strong attention to detail, a solid understanding of construction quality standards, and experience carrying out inspections, audits, and compliance checks on live sites, ensuring works are delivered to the highest standards. Candidates will be primarily situated office based in Dublin 12\. However, they also must be willing to work across multiple live project locations. **Role and Responsibilities** * Develop Quality Systems to meet business needs. * Support internal auditing, carry out site\-based QAQC Audits. * Compile reports and audits using company’s construction software. * Document and implement quality procedures on all sites and departments. * Investigate non\-conformances and corrective actions. * Mentor staff and promote skill development through training others on company procedures. * Collaborate across teams and develop quality inspection check sheets. * Maintain and review reports and inspections on the company software platform. * Support customer complaints and product reviews. * Assisting with BC(a)R and project handovers. * Carry out additional tasks as assigned by QAQC Manager. * Foster a culture of quality and support QAQC expertise across the company through training and support. **Requirements** * Trades background / 3rd level Construction Management Qualification. * 3\-5\+ years’ experience in a similar role. * Knowledge of construction best practices. * Strong communication skills. * Experience in large\-scale projects. * Familiarity with building regulations and certification standards. * Detail\-oriented and methodical. * Effective team player, self\-motivated, able to work independently. * Experience working with Procore construction software. * Lean Construction qualification/experience is an advantage.

title: Quantitative Campus Recruiter
description: **Overview** Susquehanna is a global quantitative trading firm powered by scientific rigor, curiosity, and innovation. Our culture is intellectually driven and highly collaborative, bringing together researchers, engineers, and traders to design and deploy impactful strategies in our systematic trading environment. To meet the unique challenges of global markets, Susquehanna applies machine learning and advanced quantitative research to vast datasets in order to uncover actionable insights and build effective strategies. By uniting deep market expertise with cutting\-edge technology, we excel in solving complex problems and pushing boundaries together. Our in\-house campus recruiting team is expanding! We are looking for a Quantitative Recruiter to join our quant recruiting team at our European headquarters in Dublin. If you are an individual who is passionate about mathematical problem solving and building social connections, then we want to hear from you! You will join a team of recruiters focused on hiring top\-tier talent into Susquehanna’s Quantitative Research and Quantitative Systematic Trading internships and grad programmes. Beyond interviewing and hiring, you will assist with the running of our summer internship and use data to refine our recruiting processes and strategies. We are looking for an innovative problem solver who can leverage new tools and ideas to strengthen our brand recognition, diversify our recruiting efforts, and attract the best talent. **What You’ll Do** * Leverage your mathematical background while interviewing and assessing quantitative campus candidates by working through probability, statistics, and logic questions. * Find creative ways to scale our campus efforts and attract top talent from universities across Europe. * Build and maintain strong relationships with candidates, hiring managers, and interviewers throughout the hiring process. * Attend and facilitate careers fairs, campus events, and on\-site events for our programmes. * Apply an analytical approach – leverage data to inform recruiting decisions and improve the hiring process. * Help onboard your hires and be involved in the running of the summer internship and graduate programmes. **What We're Looking For** * Bachelor’s degree in mathematics, engineering, physics, or computer science with coursework in probability, statistics, and combinatorics. * Experience with Excel preferred, but not essential. Basic programming experience also beneficial. * Strong appetite to learn how to interview students by using quantitative concepts. * Excellent written and verbal communication skills. * Strong sense of urgency and emotional intelligence. * Strategic thinker with strong analytical skills. **What’s In It For You** Our culture enables you to drive meaningful impact, by leveraging your expertise and taking ownership. Private healthcare and onsite gym, plus wellness initiatives. Fully stocked kitchen, with daily breakfast and lunch. Regular social, sporting and community events including annual poker tournament. Matched donations and corporate discounts. Seasonal opportunities to travel for recruiting events in Ireland, the UK and mainland Europe. **About Susquehanna** Susquehanna is a global quantitative trading firm powered by scientific rigor, curiosity, and innovation. Our culture is intellectually driven and highly collaborative, bringing together researchers, engineers, and traders to design and deploy impactful strategies in our systematic trading environment. To meet the unique challenges of global markets, Susquehanna applies machine learning and advanced quantitative research to vast datasets in order to uncover actionable insights and build effective strategies. By uniting deep market expertise with cutting\-edge technology, we excel in solving complex problems and pushing boundaries together. **What We Do** Susquehanna’s deep integration of trading, technology and quant research makes us experts in trading essentially all listed financial products and asset classes, with a focus on derivatives. We handle millions of trading transactions around the world every day as both a market maker and market taker. Our efforts provide liquidity and ensure competitive prices for buyers and sellers. While our presence in the market is broad, our trading desks are highly specialized to allow for a deep understanding of the unique drivers of each asset class. *If you're a recruiting agency and want to partner with us, please reach out to recruiting@sig.com. Any resume or referral submitted in the absence of a signed agreement will not be eligible for an agency fee.*

title: Quantitative Data Engineer | Options | Experienced Hire
description: **Overview** **Quantitative Data Engineer** At Susquehanna, the frontier of trading is no longer just speed—it’s also about mastering data. The Quantitative Engineering and Data group in Europe is a rapidly expanding group focused on creating algorithms, processes and datasets that enable revenue generation from data at Susquehanna. We partner with Quantitative Researchers, Traders and other engineering teams in London, Dublin and across the globe. We are looking for world leading talent that has the skills to write great Python code and navigate complex data and models. **Job Summary** You will work closely with Quantitative Research and Trading to optimise quant code and algorithms, analyse data, and develop modelling features for research. As you develop in the role you will gain an intuition into the needs of a quantitative trading business. This intuition coupled with your skills will enable you to deliver real\-world value to the company. * Member of the Quantitative Engineering and Data group * Responsible for enabling research and trading activities using a range of data science, analysis, and engineering skills. * Write, test, and deploy Python code that creates new model features, analyses complex datasets, optimizes algorithms/quant code, and defines data orchestration. * Partner with researchers on revenue generating projects taking on coding tasks that optimise project delivery. * Develop expertise in Susquehanna’s systems and business. **What We're Looking For** * Degree in a STEM subject with evidence of independent work either from work experience, Masters, or PhD. * 4\+ years professional experience in Python with practical experience solving programming problems through algorithm design. * 3\+ years hands\-on experience working with data manipulation libraries (e.g. Pandas, Polars, PySpark). * Experience working in a Linux environment. * A desire to work closely with Researchers and accelerate their research. * The ability to self\-manage, self\-motivate, and seek process improvement opportunities. * Strong interpersonal and communication skills that enable you to collaborate effectively in a quantitative domain. * Attention to detail and ability to react to changing priorities. * A general interest in quantitative trading. **About Susquehanna** Susquehanna is a global quantitative trading firm powered by scientific rigor, curiosity, and innovation. Our culture is intellectually driven and highly collaborative, bringing together researchers, engineers, and traders to design and deploy impactful strategies in our systematic trading environment. To meet the unique challenges of global markets, Susquehanna applies machine learning and advanced quantitative research to vast datasets in order to uncover actionable insights and build effective strategies. By uniting deep market expertise with cutting\-edge technology, we excel in solving complex problems and pushing boundaries together. Susquehanna is an equal opportunity employer. If you need assistance and/or a reasonable accommodation due to a disability during the application or recruitment process, please contact us. If you're a recruiting agency and want to partner with us, please reach out to recruiting@sig.com. Any resume or referral submitted in the absence of a signed agreement will not be eligible for an agency fee.

title: Quantitative Sports Researcher | Experienced Hire
description: **Overview** At Susquehanna, the Sports Analytics team builds statistical forecasting models that help provide liquidity for the sports betting industry. These models are vital to creating new trading strategies and informing decision\-makers about the distribution of game events. By joining our Sports Analytics team, you will be part of a group that is passionate about mathematics, gaming, and technology. **In This Role, You Will** * Immerse yourself in sports analytics and quantitative trading research * Empirically test ideas using real industry data * Write programs to implement models, and strategies * Collaborate with fellow researchers, traders, and technologists * Spend time trading to understand how models are used in practice and how traders think and operate, ensuring model development is grounded in real\-world insight * Occasionally provide off\-hour coverage, which may include evening or weekend shifts, while developing this understanding and when deploying or testing new models / strategies **What We're Looking For** * Strong quantitative and analytical skills, with a background in Mathematics, Physics, Statistics, Electrical Engineering, Computer Science, Operations Research, or Economics (advanced degree a strong plus but not required) * Excellent logical reasoning and problem\-solving skills * Exceptional communication skills, with the ability to both understand others and clearly express your ideas * Experience writing code to process and analyze large sets of data * Demonstrated experience working on sports analytics projects or other quantitative projects * Familiarity with sports such as NFL, NBA, MLB, NHL, NCAAF, NCAAM, soccer, tennis, or golf, as well as trading experience, is a plus but not required * Intermittent international travel may be required If you're a recruiting agency and want to partner with us, please reach out to recruiting@sig.com. Any resume or referral submitted in the absence of a signed agreement will not be eligible for an agency fee.

title: Records & Data Entry Admin
description: At Iron Mountain we know that work, when done well, makes a positive impact for our customers, our employees, and our planet. That’s why we need smart, committed people to join us. Whether you’re looking to start your career or make a change, talk to us and see how you can elevate the power of your work at Iron Mountain. We provide expert, sustainable solutions in records and information management, digital transformation services, data centers, asset lifecycle management, and fine art storage, handling, and logistics. We proudly partner every day with our 225,000 customers around the world to preserve their invaluable artifacts, extract more from their inventory, and protect their data privacy in innovative and socially responsible ways. Are you curious about being part of our growth stor y while evolving your skills in a culture that will welcome your unique contributions? If so, let's start the conversation. **Job Description** Iron Mountain's digital scanning business provides leading\-edge solutions engineered to transform physical records into accessible and secure digital information. Our expertise encompasses the meticulous conversion of paper documents into high\-quality digital assets, enabling organisations to free up valuable physical space and vastly improve information retrieval. This comprehensive service is underpinned by rigorous quality assurance protocols and indexing, ensuring that digitised documents deliver enhanced data integrity and fully comply with relevant regulatory frameworks. Through these advanced scanning and digital storage capabilities, Iron Mountain empowers businesses to support remote and hybrid workforces, streamline operations, mitigate risks associated with physical records, and unlock the full potential of their information. **Job Summary** Iron Mountain is seeking a detail\-oriented Records \& Data Entry Specialist to join our Records Management team in **Whitehall Dublin 9, D09 T9V6\.** This role is crucial to our new contract supporting the digitalisation of property records in Ireland. **This is a 12 month fixed term contract.** In this role, you will be responsible for the accurate cross\-referencing and validation of critical legal documents, ensuring they are correctly prepared and signed off for the subsequent scanning process. **Your Role In Our Mission** * Conduct meticulous cross\-referencing of document contents within each box of deeds against established manifests to ensure all correct documents are present and ready for digitalisation. * Perform essential data entry tasks and sign\-off procedures to accurately track and reconcile document status, ensuring full compliance with internal quality standards and regulatory requirements. * Diligently track and report any discrepancies, missing documents, or data errors, escalating issues to supervisors promptly to maintain data integrity and project timelines. * Provide constructive feedback on document preparation and workflow processes to leadership, helping to refine standard operating procedures for greater efficiency and accuracy across the team. * Perform light manual handling tasks, moving and organising boxes of deeds within the records facility, and collaborate effectively with scanning and warehouse operations teams to maintain a smooth workflow. **Valued Skills And Experience** * Demonstrated experience in a document handling, data entry, administrative, or warehouse operations environment. * Exceptional attention to detail, with a proven title: Recruitment Consultant – Full Training Provided
description: **Recruitment Consultant – Full Training Provided** * **Personalised Training** – No recruitment experience needed * **Structured Career Path** – Ongoing learning and development * **Unlimited Growth Potential** – Dedicated mentorship and coaching Ready for a career change where you’re in control and your progression has no limits? At Archer, we offer ambitious professionals the opportunity to transform their careers as Recruitment Consultants in a high\-growth, high\-impact environment. **Why Join Us:** * **Personalised Training** \- A structured programme designed to accelerate your career transition. No recruitment experience required \- we’ll take you from zero to expert in under 12 months. * **Structured Career Path** \- A clear, proven roadmap from Associate to Director, with 12 achievable promotions and full transparency at every stage of your progression. * **Unlimited Growth Potential** \- Ongoing mentorship, 1:1 coaching, and continuous development from day one. We invest in your long\-term success \- with no ceiling on your learning or career progression. **What You Will Do:** * **Become a Niche IT Specialist** \- Develop deep market expertise through our comprehensive training programme and position yourself as a trusted authority in your field. * **Partner with Industry Leaders** \- Work with clients across Ireland’s key sectors, including pharmaceutical, medical devices, and financial services, delivering top IT talent to build high\-performing tech teams. * **Master the Full Recruitment Cycle** \- Learn to manage client and candidate relationships, negotiate and close deals, grow accounts, and become a pivotal driver of business success. **What You Will Have:** * **Professional Experience** – 3\+ years’ post\-education experience in any field. * **Work Eligibility** \- EU Passport or Stamp 4 visa, and currently based in Dublin or planning to relocate. * **Growth Mindset** \- Ambitious, career\-driven, and motivated to thrive in a high\-performance, learning\-focused environment. **Additional Reward \& Benefits:** * **Lucrative Earnings \-** Uncapped bonus and commission, fully performance\-based, giving you control over your income. * **Performance Rewards \-** Exclusive incentives, including annual all\-inclusive ski trips and quarterly high\-performance lunches. * **Team Culture \& Events** \- Regular team outings and activities to celebrate success and strengthen connections. **Average Total Earnings Year 1 \- €47,000\+, Year 2: €75,000\+** **(** Base Salary \& Bonus€38k) **Office Based Mon\-Fri (** 8:30 am \-6 pm) For more information, contact **Saoirse.Lawton@archer.ie or** Phone \+353 (0\)1 649 8517

title: Recruitment Day - Customer Assistant - Clane
description: **Summary** From our Head Office to the shop floor and everywhere in between our primary goal is to create and deliver a fantastic customer journey for everyone who shops at Lidl. **Come along to our Recruitment Day in the Westgrove Clane on Saturday 27th Of June from 9am \- 12pm.** We will be holding fast tracked interviews with job offers being made on the day. All attendees must apply to this job before attending the Recruitment Day. Please bring a copy of your CV with you. **Customer obsessed, high energy and love learning every day?** If that sounds like you, earn €460\+ per week as a Customer Assistant on our standard 30 hour contract. You’ll work across different shift times, on varied tasks and with different colleagues. What does a normal day\* as a Customer Assistant look like? (\*there’s no such thing as ‘normal’ in retail but we’ve tried our best!) As a Customer Assistant you are required to sell alcohol, therefore you must be 18 or over to work in our store **Shift Patterns:** * Morning shift: Starting at 5am or 6am with your team, you’ll be getting our award\-winning products fully stocked on the shop floor and our delicious bakery products ready for our customers to enjoy by opening time so they can Go Full Lidl! * Middle shift: Starting mid\-morning or early afternoon, you’ll make sure our stores are spotlessly clean, well presented and our products remain fresh, all while helping customers with queries on the shop floor and working on tills to minimise till queues * Late shift: From your start time in the afternoon, you’ll make sure our customers experience is top class by serving them with a smile while on the tills, restocking missing items and getting the store ready for the next day. Customer Assistant look like? (\*there’s no such thing as ‘normal’ in retail but we’ve tried our best!) As a Customer Assistant you are required to sell alcohol, therefore you must be 18 or over to work in our store **Shift Patterns:** * Morning shift: Starting at 5am or 6am with your team, you’ll be getting our award\-winning products fully stocked on the shop floor and our delicious bakery products ready for our customers to enjoy by opening time so they can Go Full Lidl! * Middle shift: Starting mid\-morning or early afternoon, you’ll make sure our stores are spotlessly clean, well presented and our products remain fresh, all while helping customers with queries on the shop floor and working on tills to minimise till queues * Late shift: From your start time in the afternoon, you’ll make sure our customers experience is top class by serving them with a smile while on the tills, restocking missing items and getting the store ready for the next day. **What You'll Do** * Interacting with the customer in a pleasant, friendly and helpful manner * Ensure stock loss controls are followed in all areas of the store * Maintaining store and external cleanliness and hygiene standards * Following freshness and rotation principles * Ensuring all waste is managed correctly * Assisting in the stock count process **What You'll Need** * A can\-do attitude and excellent customer service skills * The willingness to go the extra mile for our customers * To be responsible and reliable * To enjoy working in a fast\-paced, varied environment * A good team player * Preferably, previous experience in a customer facing role but this is not essential provided you have the right attitude **What You'll Receive** We offer a competitive and transparent salary system, that ensures pay equality across all positions at Lidl * €15\.40 rising to €17\.90 per hour after 3 years (supplementary pay outlined below) * Unsocial hours worked (12am to 7am) * 20 days holidays per annum pro rata * Company pension * Genuine opportunities for career development * Individuals recognised as high performing will be invited to join our Leadership Academy as part of your on\-going development * Circle K and Private Health Insurance discounts available for all employees * Bike to Work Scheme * Mobile and broadband discounts with Three network * Maternity \& Paternity Leave top up, Marriage Leave, Employee Assistance Programme **Year 1** * Basic Rate €15\.40 * €19\.25 (Unsocial title: Residential Solar PV Technical Expert
description: **Residential Solar PV Technical Expert** Infinite Energy are a leading Irish solar PV company based in Carlow serving Ireland and Mainland Europe. Our residential division focuses on providing solar PV and battery storage to homes and small businesses across Ireland. **Job Title** **Residential Solar PV Technical Expert** **Job Summary** To assist with our growing residential division, Infinite Energy are seeking a Solar PV technical expert. Joining this growing team and reporting to the Residential Project Manager, the ideal candidate will have experience in fast paced environment, have strong teamwork skills, have an aptitude for problem solving and an ability to communicate well with both customers and colleagues. **Key Responsibilities** · Working closely with the Residential Project Manager regarding customer queries and issues. · Assessment of queries in a timely manner, prioritising these issues, and identifying the level of service required to close out these queries. · Close out queries that do not require the service of one of our electricians, using your strong understanding of Solar PV systems, applications, and technical knowledge. This may be done over the phone or arranging a call out. · Assessing queries for revenue potential, checking installation dates, warranty dates etc. and communicating same to both the customer and our accounts department. Where reworks/callouts are billable, identify and communicate these to the customer and the wider team. · Assist in the development of our in\-house query resolution system – implementing associated Standard Operating Procedures as and when required. · Working closely with management, wider team and customers to ensure full customer satisfaction on completion/close out of issues. · Ensuring and assisting with continuous improvement and process improvements relating to all areas noted above. · Other ad hoc duties as and when required by the company. **Qualifications \& Experience** · Experience in a similar or related role preferred but not essential. · Technical knowledge of Solar PV systems is highly desirable. An aptitude for problem solving and resolving ‘tech’ queries on PV inverters, EV chargers (via the physical product and the associated application). · Strong communications skills – an ability to diagnose and address customer queries over the phone is pivotal to this role. Someone with an ability to communicate technical information to non\-technical customers would be well suited to this role. · Experience working with customers with a high level of customer service. · Willingness to learn new processes and development of same as and when required. · Strong time management, ability to work both within a team and independently, and within a fast paced and busy environment. **Key Competencies** · Strong organisational and planning skills with a focus on detail. · Ability to work independently with an aptitude for problem solving. · Strong communication skills – both internally and intenally. · Strong IT skills (Excel, stock systems, reporting tools) · Quality and Customer Service focussed. · Proactive and solution driven. · Ability to work in a fast\-paced, growing environment. · Team player with good communication skills. Salary dependent on experience. Pay: €40,000\.00\-€45,000\.00 per year Work Location: In person

title: Retail Artist
description: **Retail Artist \- Brown Thomas, Dundrum** **Part\-time position** **About Charlotte Tilbury Beauty** *Founded by British makeup artist and beauty entrepreneur Charlotte Tilbury MBE in 2013, Charlotte Tilbury Beauty has revolutionised the face of the global beauty industry by de\-coding makeup applications for everyone, everywhere, with an easy\-to\-use, easy\-to\-choose, easy\-to\-gift range. Today, Charlotte Tilbury Beauty continues to break records across countries, channels, and categories and to scale at pace.* *Over the last 10 years, Charlotte Tilbury Beauty has experienced exceptional growth and is one of the most talked about brands in the beauty industry and beyond. It has become a global sensation across 50 markets (and growing), with over 2,300 employees globally who are part of the Dream Team making the magic happen.* *Today, Charlotte Tilbury Beauty is a truly global business, delivering market\-leading growth, innovative retail and product launches fuelled by industry\-leading tech — all with an internal culture of embracing challenges, disruptive thinking, winning together, and sharing the magic. The energy behind the bran­d is infectious, and as we grow, we are always looking for extraordinary talent who want to be part of this our success and help drive our limitless ambitions.* **About The Role** * You can work at a fast pace to achieve and exceed KPI's. You feed on the buzz of achieving your goals and being the best, you can be. You do not want to meet expectation \- you exceed it * "Team work makes the dream work" \- is your working motto. You understand your impact and how vital your role is. You can adapt to change at the last minute and be a real support to your managers and colleagues alike * You champion the art of world\-class artistry and an impeccable customer experience. You are a Charlotte Tilbury brand ambassador and want to pass on your knowledge to customer after customer. You live and breathe the Charlotte Tilbury makeup looks \- and can recreate the magic of these on your customer, as well as offering advice and guidance. You pride yourself on knowing your customers are returning back to you * You follow the company grooming standards to a tee \- you know what good looks like and are not afraid to display this **About You** * You will ideally have had some experience as a Sales Advisor. Ideally this will be gained from a fast\-paced environment such as beauty, retail or FMCG * You aspire to be part of a beauty brand that disrupts the way it is seen \- and continues to grow. You love all things Charlotte Tilbury \- and are not afraid to spread the word * Ideally you will have experience in applying makeup \- and knowing how to adapt your style to different customers with different needs * You champion positivity \- and can think in a limitless way that makes your energy shine ***Why join us?*** * You can only be the best when you are set up to succeed \- not only do we offer on the job training. We offer you the opportunity to attend Charlotte's Magic Academy \- where dreams are truly made. Combining art, commerce and Charlotte's own values. You will feel truly empowered to live the magic in your role * Opportunities to disrupt processes (in a positive way!) and have your voice truly heard * The opportunity to be part of a company where we celebrate our achievements, and you are included. With a real scope for growth and progression to support your own development * You receive exclusive launches of our incredible products before anyone else \- not only that you even receive a full Charlotte Tilbury look once you pass your probation. (That is right all the products from one of our iconic looks) * We also offer an incredible staff discount, and access to Tilbury Treats \- our very own rewards platform allowing you to save money and gain money cannot buy discounts on anything from gym memberships to cinema tickets *At Charlotte Tilbury Beauty, our mission is to empower everybody in the world to be the most beautiful version of themselves. We celebrate and support this by encouraging and hiring people with diverse backgrounds, cultures, voices, beliefs, and perspectives into our growing global workforce. By doing so, we better serve our communities, customers, employees \- and the candidates that take part in our recruitment process.* *If you want to learn more about life at Charlotte Tilbury Beauty please follow our* *LinkedIn page!*

title: Retail IT Support Analyst
description: Job Description The Retail IT Support Analyst keeps ARI’s retail technology running smoothly by delivering fast, effective support across systems, devices and end users. The role also drives continuous improvement by supporting projects, managing equipment and documenting business process changes, while deputising for the IT Manager when needed. Responsibilities * Provide first\-line support for ARI retail systems and end users, resolving incidents quickly and restoring service to minimise disruption to store operations. * Troubleshoot, diagnose, and coordinate resolution of POS, eCommerce, HHT and related hardware/software issues, ensuring stable and secure retail technology performance. * Log, prioritise, track and communicate progress on tickets, escalating effectively to internal specialist teams or external support partners to meet agreed service levels. * Identify recurring issues and trends, and implement or recommend preventative fixes to reduce repeat incidents and improve the end\-user experience. * Support and maintain retail IT equipment lifecycle activities (setup, configuration, replacements and basic inventory coordination) to keep stores operational and compliant with standards. * Partner with retail users to document business process and system change requirements, translating operational needs into clear technical inputs for delivery through the change request process. * Provide user guidance and informal training on systems, applications and best practices, enabling colleagues to use tools effectively and reducing avoidable support requests. * Assist the IT Manager with ongoing retail projects and deputise as required, ensuring continuity of day\-to\-day support and progress on agreed deliverables (no direct people management or budget ownership). Qualifications **Qualifications** Bachelor's degree in Information Technology, Computer Science, or a related field, or equivalent work experience. **Experience** 2\+ years of IT support experience, preferably in a retail or customer\-facing environment. Experience supporting POS systems, retail hardware, and end\-user systems. Project Management Experience assisting with or managing small IT projects, particularly in a retail setting – desirable but not essential We're an airport industry leader with operations in four continents and over 3000 employees working together to ensure we deliver the best services for all our customers globally. We are a fast paced, energetic and diverse company. Offering careers that range from IT, Finance, Technical, Airport Operations to frontline security and customer focused roles and many more. Whether you are starting your career or looking for your next move, we have lots of great opportunities to choose from. ARI is one of the world’s longest established and experienced global travel retail companies. With expertise in developing business in new and existing markets, ARI owns and manages retail outlets in 27 airports in 14 countries throughout Europe, The Middle East, Asia Pacific, and North America. From opening the world’s first duty free in Shannon Airport in 1947, ARI now employs over 3,500 people worldwide and manages a turnover in excess of €1 billion. ARI is part of the Irish government\-owned daa group.

title: Retail Mobile Cleaner
description: **Company Description** Sports betting, gaming and interactive entertainment is changing, and we’re leading that change. By putting people first. By placing exciting, engaging and entertaining experiences at more fingertips than ever before. We're pulling into pole position by pushing boundaries further. With innovation. With technology. But most importantly, with people like you. Because when you join Entain, it’s your game. So let’s win together. **Job Description** We are currently looking for a Mobile Cleaner to join the team at Entain we pride ourselves on Service and Standards in our shops so you will be completing deep cleans and emergency cleans of our shops in the region. We are looking for reliable and experienced cleaners with high standards and dedication to provide our shop teams with excellent customer service! Reporting to the Cleaning Operations Manager you will be part of the Regional Cleaning Team, who will deep clean and complete immediate response to urgent cleaning requests. **What You Will Do** Deep cleans are 2 hours in length, are completed twice a year and include: * Carpet washing/Hardwood floor cleaning * Cleaning TV Screens and Casing * Cleaning furniture * Cleaning Gaming Machine Area. * Cleaning Customer and Staff Toilets * Cleaning Staff Area * Emergency cleans are on request and the reasons for cleaning vary, they require an immediate response and are prioritised ahead of other deep cleaning tasks ****Working patterns**** As a Mobile cleaner you will work 5 out of 7 days a week. **Qualifications** * Proven working experience as a cleaner. * Full UK Driving Licence (clean). * Knowledge of cleaning materials and supplies. * Knowledge of health and safety cleaning regulations. * Ability to prioritise urgent tasks. * Excellent time keeping and time management. **Additional Information** At Entain, we know that signing top players requires a great starting package, and plenty of support to inspire peak performance. Join us, and a competitive salary is just the beginning. Working for us across the region, you can expect to receive great benefits like: * Access to a company vehicle \& fuel card * Optional overtime subject to availability * Discounts to over 500 different retailers * Pension Scheme \& Annual share save * Enrolment in our bonus scheme And outside of this, you’ll have the chance to turn recognition from leaders and colleagues into amazing prizes. Join a winning team of talented people and be a part of an inclusive and supporting community where everyone is celebrated for being themselves. *Should you need any adjustments or accommodations to the recruitment process, at either application or interview, please contact us.* **Equal Opportunities.** If you need any reasonable adjustments at any stage of the recruitment process, please contact us and we'll support you. We're committed to creating a diverse, equitable and inclusive workplace where everyone feels valued, respected and able to be themselves. We're an equal opportunities employer. We welcome applications from everyone and we do not discriminate based on race, colour, nationality, ethnic or national origin, religion or belief, sex, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, age, disability, marital or civil partnership status, pregnancy or maternity, or any other status protected by law. We comply with all applicable recruitment regulations and employment laws in the jurisdictions where we operate, ensuring ethical and compliant hiring practices globally.

title: Sales Development Representative (French Speaker)
description: **Company Overview** -------------------- Docusign brings agreements to life. Over 1\.5 million customers and more than a billion people in over 180 countries use Docusign solutions to accelerate the process of doing business and simplify people’s lives. With intelligent agreement management, Docusign unleashes business\-critical data that is trapped inside of documents. Until now, these were disconnected from business systems of record, costing businesses time, money, and opportunity. Using Docusign’s Intelligent Agreement Management platform, companies can create, commit, and manage agreements with solutions created by the \#1 company in e\-signature and contract lifecycle management (CLM). **What You'll Do** ------------------ Are you an agile, creative, and collaborative Sales Development Representative looking for your first break within Sales, or a chance to grow your Sales career in the tech industry? We can help. You are the driver of building new business relationships, connecting with potential clients who have expressed interest in Docusign and targeting those you identify as having an opportunity to work with. This position is an individual contributor role reporting to the Sales Development Manager, EMEA. **Responsibility** * Spend about 80% of your time on the phone contacting prospective businesses identified as having real potential as a Docusign customer from an inbound lead flow * Educate potential customers on what Docusign has to offer and gain insight into their business needs * Use targeted questions and your natural rapport building skills to understand the client's business strategy and how we at Docusign can contribute to their success * Provide qualified, informative leads in our CRM database for Account Executives to pursue, and initial liaison scheduling **Job Designation** ------------------- **Hybrid:** Employee divides their time between in\-office and remote work. Access to an office location is required. (Frequency: Minimum 2 days per week; may vary by team but will be weekly in\-office expectation) Positions at Docusign are assigned a job designation of either In Office, Hybrid or Remote and are specific to the role/job. Preferred job designations are not guaranteed when changing positions within Docusign. Docusign reserves the right to change a position's job designation depending on business needs and as permitted by local law. **What You Bring** ------------------ **Basic** * Experience with Salesforce CRM, Google Workspace, and other core business applications * Fluency in French and English **Preferred** * You have excellent communication and interpersonal skills: You love engaging with new people and companies and thrive on making successful connections using your initiative, introducing companies to a product you are passionate about * A multi\-tasker, you enjoy a busy, challenging role where you can take ownership of your success and contribute to a driven team * Ability to quickly learn and adopt new technologies in a fast\-paced, Al\-first environment * Track record of success in inbound sales/business development role * Confident and courteous verbal and written communication skills * Excellent organizational skills * Familiarity with Al\-powered sales, research, and productivity tools, including Gong, Glean, Gemini, Notebook LM * Bachelor's degree **Life At Docusign** -------------------- **Working here** Docusign is committed to building trust and making the world more agreeable for our employees, customers and the communities in which we live and work. You can count on us to listen, be honest, and try our best to do what’s right, every day. At Docusign, everything is equal. We each have a responsibility to ensure every team member has an equal opportunity to succeed, to be heard, to exchange ideas openly, to build lasting relationships, and to do the work of their life. Best of all, you will be able to feel deep pride in the work you do, because your contribution helps us make the world better than we found it. And for that, you’ll be loved by us, our customers, and the world in which we live. **Accommodation** Docusign is committed to providing reasonable accommodations for qualified individuals with disabilities in our job application procedures. If you need such an accommodation, or a religious accommodation, during the application process, please contact us at accommodations@docusign.com. If you experience any issues, concerns, or technical difficulties during the application process please get in touch with our Talent organization at taops@docusign.com for assistance.
